title: High-throughput Sars-CoV-2  detection from self-collected saline gargle samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5kft5","text":"The corona virus disease 19 (COVID19) pandemic, caused by severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) has caused a worldwide health crisis. A rigid test, trace and isolate strategy is obstructed by a global shortage in lab supplies such as RNA extraction kits, RT-PCR reagents or other consumables. We opted to develop a cheap and easily scalable molecular Sars-CoV-2 test.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4i3ja","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e446p","text":"Nasopharanygeal and/or throat swabs and subsequent quantitative RT-PCR (RT-qPCR) are the current gold standard for detection of SARS-CoV-2. Swabs need to be taken, however, by skilled personell and exposure to infected individuals poses a serious health risk to health care professionals. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9seme","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eq80b","text":"Here, we describe a protocol for high-throughput Sars-CoV-2 detection from self-collected saline gargle samples, a rapid (collection to result \u003c 3 hours), scalable (up to several 1'000 samples per day) and sensitive (10 copies Sars-CoV-2 per reaction) molecular test that based on self-collected saliva. Unstimulated saliva after wake-up or fasting for 1 hour is collected by gargling with common saline (0.9% NaCl) solution. Saliva is stored in plastic buckets without additives and is stable even after 12 hours at 4°C plus at least one freeze-thaw cycle. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"81nm4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"879b5","text":"When compared to the best available method for Sars-CoV-2 detection, our protocol reaches a positive percent agreement of 95 % and a negative percent agreement of 98.9 % at a cost of ca. 5 $ per test.  Rapidly scalable, molecular detection of Sars-CoV-2 from saline/saliva mixtures using alternative reagents could help reduce disease burden when implemented in a test, trace and isolate regimen. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dq9ea","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"81ple","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6d8u3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fgsnq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1qptn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cr2bm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ddfkh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"agvjk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"4b982","text":"Sample collection for validation of samples was performed in accordance with Swiss legislation in the framework of the ACCURATE-BL-COVID-19 study (https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT04625257).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":147,"length":47}],"data":{}},{"key":"49l93","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3duud","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"15f85","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e6t2p","text":"Sample collection at home (5 ml 0.9% NaCl solution, plastic collection cup):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fogck","text":"After wake-up, do not drink, eat, smoke or brush your teeth.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f8af","text":"Gargle with saline solution for 30 seconds, then collect in cup, e.g. 50 ml falcon tube. Keep at 4°C if possible.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"11spo","text":"If collection after wake-up is not possible, fasting for 1 hour (no drinking, eating, smoking) and subsequent collection is possible. CAUTION: do not gargle in public spaces due to generation of infectious aerosols.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":134,"length":81}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e3rhd","text":"For validation, our sampes were kept on ice for up to 12 hours and then frozen at -80°C until thawed on ice before processing. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e52tq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"59c9l","text":"Our test was compared with the best available method and reached a positive percent agreement of 95 % and a negative percent agreement of 98.9 %.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8us87","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"2jclj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"E8E886257D8E11EBBE440A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT04625257"}},"1":{"type":"safety","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"dc493","text":"Personal protective equipment (PPE) for sample handling is essential. Use double pair of gloves, face masks, protective glasses and lab coats.","type":"unstyled"},{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"d9sag","text":"","type":"unstyled"},{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"1evl9","text":"Every sample has to be considered as infectious!","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}},"2":{"type":"notes","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"dc493","text":"The use of 15ml Falcon tubes is not recommended due to the narrow opening. We observed  difficulties of patients to donate saliva into the 15 ml tubes. If 50 ml falcon tubes are not feasible, any other wide-bore collection cup can be used.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}}}}

warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"55cni","text":"All steps involving patient samples have to be performed in accordance with local biosafety and ethic guidelines. Saliva samples were considered hazardous - Switzerland: biosafetly level 2 - prior to 65°C inactivation for 15 minutes. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6o7dq","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"1ka4g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"safety","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"bugcd","text":"Wearing personal protective equipment *e.g. FFP2 masks, protective eyewear, two pairs of gloves, lab coat* is necessary and strongly adivised.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}}}}

steps: Prepare GdnHCl / Chelex mix. Note: GdnHCl helps to stablize the RNA at 65°C during Chelex incubation. Prepare per sample plus 15% overage: 250 µL GdnHCl 8 Molarity (M) + 900 µL Chelex 30%. CHELEX beads settle quickly and need to be resuspended directly prior to pipetting
Add saliva to Chelex / GdnHCl mix. Using a multichannel pipette, add 450 µL of saliva-saline solution to 1150 µL CHELEX / GdnHCl. Mix thouroughly.
Prepare nucleic acid extraction plate: 2 mL - 2.5 mL 96 deep well plate. Pre-fill wells with 1150 µL GdnHCl / CHELEX mix. Put on ice On ice .

Prepare SpeedBeads in SPRI RNA bead binding buffer. SpeedBeads need to be vortexed thouroughly before pipetting to ensure a good suspension For 100 µL of SpeedBeads, add 4.9 mL of SPRI RNA bead binding buffer. Bead suspension can be stored at 4 °C for 4 weeks. Prepare per sample + 15% overage 480 µL SpeedBead-Mix Put On ice
Set plate thermoshaker at 65 °C .
Seal 96 deep well plate tightly with aluminium plate seal or sealing mat. Ensure that no cross-contamination can occur during shaking
Incubate samples for 00:15:00 at 65 °C under constant shaking at 700 rpm, °C, - 900 rpm, °C, and put On ice ..
Briefly spin down 96 deep well plate at 200 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 to pellet CHELEX resin.
Transfer 600 µL of supernatant from CHELEX / GdnHCl incubation to SpeedBead-Mix, resuspend thouroughly.
Prepare a fresh 1.1 mL - 1.5 mL 96 deep well plate with 480 µL of SpeedBead-Mix per well.
Seal plate, incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature . . During incubation, prepare 1 mL fresh 80% EtOH per sample in nuclease-free water.
Put deep well plate on magnetic rack, wait for 00:05:00 or until all beads have pelleted.
Remove supernatant, add 500 µL of 80% EtOH to beads, briefly shake plate and repeat this step once. Shake plate gently to avoid cross-contamination.
Remove residual EtOH, dry plate for 00:02:00 . or until beads no longer look "shiny". Do not let beads overdry and crack
Remove from magnet, add 100 µL 10 millimolar (mM) Tris pH 8 per well. Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature . Resuspend well

Pellet on magnet for 00:05:00 . Collect up to 75 µl of supernatant in clean tubes or plates and immediately proceed with RT-qPCR. Alternatively, samples can be stored at -80°C for further use. Technical note: We observed that it may help leave some elution buffer in the plate to avoid resuspension of beads.

Prepare bead binding buffer For a 1x working solution mix following chemicals: A B C D 1 Item Conc. (stock) Volume Conc. (final) 2 Sodium Chloride 5 Molarity (M) 25 mL 2.5 Molarity (M) 3 Nuclease-free Water 4.672 mL 4 Trisodium citrate 1 Molarity (M) 50 µL 1 Milimolar (mM) 5 Tween 20 20% w/v 250 µL 0.05% volume 6 PEG 8000 50% w/v 20 mL 20% volume 7 HCl 1 Molarity (M) 28 µL 0.56 Milimolar (mM) Components for a 1x working solution for 50 mL Titrate the Buffer to pH 6.4 with HCl. Approximately 35 µL of 37% HCl for a 100 mL Volume. Link: https://openwetware.org/wiki/SPRI_bead_binding_buffer

Prepare a 30% w/v Chelex stock solution: For 16 Samples add 4.5 g Chelex to a 15 ml falcon tube. Fill to 15 mL with nuclease-free, deionized water. Shake the tube well until no clumps are visible. Chelex stock solution is stable at 4°C for up to 4 weeks. A simple direct RT-LAMP SARS-CoV-2 saliva diagnostic. medRxiv.

For the PCR step, following primers are used: A B C D E 1 Name Sequence:5'->3' Stock solution [µM] Stock solution for primer master mix [µl] Final reaction conc.[nM] 2 CDC N1 fwd GAC CCC AAA ATC AGC GAA AT 100 4 400 3 CDC N1 rev TCT GGT TAC TGC CAG TTG AAT CTG 100 4 400 4 CDC N1 FAM ACC CCG CAT TAC GTT TGG TGG ACC 100 2 200 5 Charité E-Sarbeco fwd ACA GGT ACG TTA ATA GTT AAT AGC GT 100 4 400 6 Charité E-Sarbeco rev ATA TTG CAG CAG TAC GCA CAC A 100 4 400 7 Charité E-Sarbeco HEX ACA CTA GCC ATC CTT ACT GCG CTT CG 100 2 200 8 CDC RNase P fwd AGA TTT GGA CCT GCG AGC 100 4 400 9 CDC RNase P rev GAG CGG CTG TCT CCA CAA GT 100 4 400 10 CDC RNase P Cy5 TTC TGA CCT GAA GGC TCT GCG CG 100 2 200 Add 70 µl nuclease-free H 2 O. Stocks can be kept at 100 uM. Analytical sensitivity and efficiency comparisons of SARS-CoV-2 RT-qPCR primer-probe sets.. Nature microbiology. Detection of SARS-CoV-2 RNA by multiplex RT-qPCR.. PLoS biology.

Following table is for one PCR reaction: A B 1 TaqPath™ 1-Step Multiplex Master Mix (No ROX) 2.5 µl 2 Primer Mix (step 17) 1 µl 3 Nuclease-free water 1.5 µl 4 Sample 5 µl 5 Total 10 µl Prepare On ice . The primers are light sensitive. Avoid long exposure to light. Cover up with aluminum foil. Use RNAse + nucleic acid free tubes.

Load onto a RT-PCR plate - we usually run all samples and controls in duplicates. Note: we used 500 copies of TWIST synthetic RNA as positive control and nuclease-free water as negative control. Spin down quickly before loading the plate into the RT-PCR machine.

PCR Program used will take 01:20:00 : UNC incubation and Hold Stage: 25 °C 00:02:00 53 °C 00:10:00 95 °C 00:02:00 PCR Stage: 95 °C 00:00:10 55 °C 00:00:30 d Repeat for 45 cycles. Analytical sensitivity and efficiency comparisons of SARS-CoV-2 RT-qPCR primer-probe sets.. Nature microbiology.

Interpretation of results A B C D 1 E Ct (both replicates) N1 Ct (both replicates) RNase P Ct (both replicates) Interpretation 2 ≤ 40 ≤ 40 positive 3 any any ≥ 35 invalid 4 > 40 > 40 negative Interpretation guideline: - positive test result : all four Sars-CoV-2 genes, e.g. both E and both N1 replicates, must show Ct values < 40. - negative test result: between 0 and 3 Sars-CoV-2 genes with Ct values < 40 AND RNase P Ct < 35.
creator: Karl Frontzek
